examples.title: Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle Simulation
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how the CPU fetches, decodes, and executes instructions
examples.code:
// SIMPLE FETCH-DECODE-EXECUTE CYCLE
BEGIN
  // Initialize
  PC = START_ADDRESS
  IR = 0
  
  WHILE (NOT HALT) DO
    // FETCH PHASE
    MAR = PC              // Load address into Memory Address Register
    READ_MEMORY           // Fetch instruction from memory
    IR = MDR              // Load instruction into Instruction Register
    PC = PC + 1          // Increment Program Counter
    
    // DECODE PHASE
    OPCODE = IR[15:12]    // Extract opcode from instruction
    OPERAND = IR[11:0]    // Extract operand address
    CONTROL_SIGNALS = DECODE(OPCODE)
    
    // EXECUTE PHASE
    SWITCH (OPCODE) DO
      CASE LOAD:
        MAR = OPERAND
        READ_MEMORY
        ACC = MDR
      CASE STORE:
        MAR = OPERAND
        MDR = ACC
        WRITE_MEMORY
      CASE ADD:
        MAR = OPERAND
        READ_MEMORY
        ACC = ACC + MDR
      CASE HALT:
        HALT = TRUE
    END SWITCH
  END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode simulates the basic instruction cycle. It shows how the CPU fetches an instruction from memory, decodes it to determine the operation, and then executes the appropriate action. The cycle repeats until a HALT instruction is encountered.

examples.title: CPU Register Simulation
examples.description: Python implementation showing how CPU registers work during instruction execution
examples.code:
class CPURegisters:
    def __init__(self):
        # Initialize CPU registers
        self.PC = 0        # Program Counter - holds next instruction address
        self.IR = 0        # Instruction Register - holds current instruction
        self.ACC = 0       # Accumulator - stores arithmetic results
        self.MAR = 0       # Memory Address Register
        self.MDR = 0       # Memory Data Register
        self.IR = 0        # Instruction Register
        
    def fetch_instruction(self, memory):
        """Fetch instruction from memory at PC address"""
        self.MAR = self.PC
        self.IR = memory[self.PC]
        self.PC += 1
        print(f"FETCH: Instruction {self.IR} loaded from address {self.MAR}")
        
    def decode_instruction(self):
        """Decode the instruction to get opcode and operand"""
        opcode = (self.IR >> 8) & 0xFF  # Upper 8 bits as opcode
        operand = self.IR & 0xFF       # Lower 8 bits as operand
        print(f"DECODE: Opcode={opcode}, Operand={operand}")
        return opcode, operand
    
    def execute_instruction(self, opcode, operand, memory):
        """Execute the decoded instruction"""
        if opcode == 1:    # LOAD instruction
            self.MAR = operand
            self.MDR = memory[self.MAR]
            self.ACC = self.MDR
            print(f"EXECUTE LOAD: Loaded {self.ACC} into Accumulator")
        elif opcode == 2:  # STORE instruction
            self.MAR = operand
            self.MDR = self.ACC
            memory[self.MAR] = self.MDR
            print(f"EXECUTE STORE: Stored {self.ACC} to memory address {self.MAR}")
        elif opcode == 3:  # ADD instruction
            self.MAR = operand
            self.MDR = memory[self.MAR]
            self.ACC = self.ACC + self.MDR
            print(f"EXECUTE ADD: Accumulator now = {self.ACC}")
        elif opcode == 99: # HALT instruction
            print("EXECUTE HALT: Stopping execution")
            return False
        return True
    
    def run_cycle(self, memory):
        """Execute one complete fetch-decode-execute cycle"""
        self.fetch_instruction(memory)
        opcode, operand = self.decode_instruction()
        return self.execute_instruction(opcode, operand, memory)

# Example usage
cpu = CPURegisters()
memory = [0] * 256
memory[0] = 0x0105   # LOAD from address 5
memory[1] = 0x0306   # ADD from address 6
memory[2] = 0x0207   # STORE to address 7
memory[3] = 0x6300   # HALT
memory[5] = 10       # Data at address 5
memory[6] = 15       # Data at address 6

print("Starting CPU simulation...")
while True:
    if not cpu.run_cycle(memory):
        break
print(f"\nFinal Accumulator Value: {cpu.ACC}")
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates the basic CPU register operations during instruction execution. It demonstrates how the Program Counter tracks the next instruction, how instructions are fetched into the Instruction Register, how they are decoded to extract opcode and operand, and how the Accumulator stores results during execution.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of instructions

examples.title: Memory Access Operations in C
examples.description: C program demonstrating memory read and write operations through CPU registers
examples.explanation: This C program demonstrates how a CPU interacts with memory. It simulates CPU registers (PC, IR, ACC, MAR, MDR) and shows the fetch-execute cycle. The program loads data from memory addresses, performs arithmetic using the accumulator, and stores results back to memory.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of instructions executed
